<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
正在了解 JS-DevTools/npm-publish 动作的工作原理。

已定位到 ENEEDAUTH 错误的根本原因。JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4 没有显式配置认证令牌，而 npm publish 在 GitHub Actions 中通常需要 NODE_AUTH_TOKEN 或 NPM_TOKEN 才能成功发布到 npmjs.org。

进一步排查发现，工作流虽然配置了 OIDC 的 id-token 权限，但 npm-publish@v4 可能需要显式传入 `token` 或 `provenance` 参数才能启用可信发布。目前缺少必要的认证令牌或 provenance 配置。</details></think>

By default, the only thing you need to do is set&#96;permissions.id-token&#96; to&#96;write&#96; to enable trusted publishing via OIDC.\n[...]\njobs:\n  publish:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6\n        with:\n          node-version: \"24\"\n      - run: npm ci\n      - run: npm test\n      - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n[...]\nIf you're publishing a private package with trusted publishing, you will still need to provide a read-only token so the action can read existing versions from the registry before publish.\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n  - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n+   with:\n+     token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\nYou can also publish to third-party registries\n[...]\n, to publish to the GitHub Package Registry, set&#96;token&#96; to&#96;secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN&#96; and\n[...]\nregistry&#96; to\n[...]\n.pkg.github\n[...]\n| &#96;token&#96; | string | None | Registry authentication token, not required if\n[...]\ntrusted publishing³ |\n[...]\n| &#96;registry&#96;¹ | string | &#96;https://registry.npmjs.org/&#96; | Registry URL\n[...]\n&#96;package&#96;\n[...]\nPath to a\n[...]\na&#96;package.json&#96;, or\n[...]\nas a security precaution\n[...]\n| &#96;dry-run&#96; | boolean | &#96;false&#96; | Run&#96;npm publish&#96; with the&#96;--dry-run&#96; flag to prevent\n[...]\nThe global&#96;.npmrc&#96; file is no longer read nor modified. These workarounds should be removed.\n[...]\n- - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v1\n+ - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n    with:\n      token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n\n  - name: Do some more stuff with npm\n    run: npm whoami\n    env:\n[...]\n-     INPUT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n+     NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eFrom Deprecated npm Classic Tokens to OIDC Trusted Publishing: A CI/CD Troubleshooting Journey - DEV Community\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://dev.to/zhangjintao/from-deprecated-npm-classic-tokens-to-oidc-trusted-publishing-a-cicd-troubleshooting-journey-4h8b\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e### Attempt 3: Setting NODE_AUTH_TOKEN to Empty String\n[...]\nBased on some outdated resources, I tried setting&#96;NODE_AUTH_TOKEN&#96; to an empty string:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n- name: Publish to npm\n  run: npm publish --access public\n  env:\n    NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ''\n[...]\nHere's the critical misconception: setting an empty&#96;NODE_AUTH_TOKEN&#96; actually prevents OIDC from working, because npm attempts to use the empty token instead of OIDC.\n[...]\n### Attempt 4: Completely Removing NODE_AUTH_TOKEN\n[...]\nI finally realized that for OIDC Trusted Publishing,&#96;NODE_AUTH_TOKEN&#96; should not be set at all:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n- name: Publish to npm\n  run: npm publish --access public\n  # Note: no env section\n[...]\n### 2. GitHub Actions Workflow Configuration\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nname: Release\n\non:\n  push:\n    tags:\n      - 'v*'\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write   # Required for OIDC authentication\n  contents: write   # Required for creating GitHub Release\n[...]\njobs:\n  release:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v4\n        with:\n          node-version: '22'\n          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'\n\n      - name: Update npm to latest\n        run: npm install -g npm@latest\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm ci\n\n      - name: Publish to npm\n        run: npm publish --access public\n        # Note: Do NOT set NODE_AUTH_TOKEN!\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2\n        with:\n          generate_release_notes: true\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\nDo not set NODE_AUTH_TOKEN – For OIDC, this environment variable should not be set at all\n[...]\n### Q: Why doesn't setting NODE_AUTH_TOKEN to an empty string work?\n[...]\nA: An empty string is still a value—npm will attempt to use it rather than falling back to OIDC. Only when this variable is completely unset will npm automatically use OIDC.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="web_search"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **web_search**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
正在分析工作流配置，以确定 npm 发布失败的原因。

已定位到根本原因。工作流中未提供有效的 npm 认证凭据。`setup-node` 虽然配置了 registry-url，但没有传入有效的 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` 秘密变量。同时 `npm-publish@v4` 也未启用 Trusted Publishing 或显式配置 provenance。因此 `npm publish` 因缺少有效凭证而返回 ENEEDAUTH 错误。

另外，npmjs.com 上可能未配置 Trusted Publishing。即使工作流启用了 id-token: write，仍需在 npm 包设置中手动信任该 GitHub 仓库。

同时，npm-publish@v4 不再读取全局 .npmrc 文件，因此必须通过显式参数或 OIDC 令牌来提供认证凭据。

还排查到几个潜在原因。

- NODE_AUTH_TOKEN 秘密变量可能为空字符串，导致 OIDC 认证失败。
- 包未使用作用域名称（如 @scope/name），首次发布时可能需要显式设置 `access: public`。
- npm-publish@v4 在某些情况下可能需要显式配置 `provenance: true` 才能启用 Trusted Publishing。</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

```json
{
  "markdown": "# Fast, easy publishing to NPM\n\n[![Build Status](https://github.com/JS-DevTools/npm-publish/workflows/CI-CD/badge.svg)](https://github.com/JS-DevTools/npm-publish/actions)\n[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/JS-DevTools/npm-publish/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/JS-DevTools/npm-publish)\n[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@jsdevtools/npm-publish.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jsdevtools/npm-publish)\n[![License](https://img.shields.io/npm/l/@jsdevtools/npm-publish.svg)](LICENSE)\n[![Buy us a tree](https://img.shields.io/badge/Treeware-%F0%9F%8C%B3-lightgreen)](https://plant.treeware.earth/JS-DevTools/npm-publish)\n\nPublish packages to npm automatically in GitHub Actions whenever a change to your package's &#96;version&#96; field is detected.\n\n- [Change log][releases]\n- [v3 to v4 migration guide](#v3-to-v4)\n- [v2 to v4 migration guide](#v2-to-v4)\n- [v1 to v4 migration guide](#v1-to-v4)\n\n[releases]: https://github.com/JS-DevTools/npm-publish/releases\n\n## ⚠️ You probably don't need this!\n\nThis action automates a specific kind of continuous deployment to &#96;npm&#96;, where you want to publish whenever the &#96;version&#96; field in &#96;package.json&#96; changes on your &#96;main&#96; branch. If you prefer to publish on tags (for example, those created by the &#96;npm version&#96; command), or are using an alternative package manager like &#96;pnpm&#96;, you don't need this action! Simply configure &#96;setup-node&#96; with its &#96;registry-url&#96; option and call your package manager's &#96;publish&#96; command directly. This is more secure than relying on a third-party action like this one, and is more customizable.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\n# Publish to npm whenever a tag is pushed\nname: Publish to npm\non:\n  push:\n    tags: v*\njobs:\n  build:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6\n        with:\n          node-version: \"24\"\n          registry-url: \"https://registry.npmjs.org\"\n      - run: npm ci\n      - run: npm test\n      - run: npm publish --ignore-scripts\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSee GitHub's [Node.js publishing][] guide and npm's [trusted publishing][] docs for more details and examples.\n\n[Node.js publishing]: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/publish-packages/publish-nodejs-packages\n[trusted publishing]: https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers#supported-cicd-providers\n\n## Features\n\n- 🧠 **Smart**\n Only publishes if the version number in &#96;package.json&#96; differs from the latest on npm.\n\n- 🛠 **Configurable**\n Customize the version-checking behavior, the registry URL, and path of your package.\n\n- 🔐 **Secure**\n Keeps your npm authentication token secret. Doesn't read nor write to &#96;~/.npmrc&#96;.\n\n- ⚡ **Fast**\n 100% JavaScript (which is faster than Docker) and bundled to optimize loading time.\n\n- 📤 **Outputs**\n Exposes the old and new version numbers, and the type of change (major, minor, patch, etc.) as variables that you can use in your workflow.\n\n## Usage\n\nThis package can be used three different ways:\n\n- 🤖 A [**GitHub Action**](#github-action) as part of your CI/CD process\n\n- 🧩 A [**function**](#javascript-api) that you call in your JavaScript code\n\n- 🖥 A [**CLI**](#command-line-interface) that you run in your terminal\n\n## GitHub Action\n\nTo use the GitHub Action, you'll need to add it as a step in your [workflow file][]. By default, the only thing you need to do is set &#96;permissions.id-token&#96; to &#96;write&#96; to enable [trusted publishing][] via OIDC.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\non:\n  push:\n    branches: main\n\njobs:\n  publish:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6\n        with:\n          node-version: \"24\"\n      - run: npm ci\n      - run: npm test\n      - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\u003e [!IMPORTANT]\n\u003e If you're publishing a private package with [trusted publishing][], you will still need to provide a read-only [&#96;token&#96;][npm authentication token] so the action can read existing versions from the registry before publish.\n\u003e\n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n\u003e - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n\u003e + with:\n\u003e + token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can also publish to third-party registries. For example, to publish to the [GitHub Package Registry][], set &#96;token&#96; to &#96;secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN&#96; and &#96;registry&#96; to &#96;https://npm.pkg.github.com&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\non:\n  push:\n    branches: main\n\njobs:\n  publish:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write # allow GITHUB_TOKEN to publish packages\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6\n        with:\n          node-version: \"24\"\n      - run: npm ci\n      - run: npm test\n      - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n        with:\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          registry: \"https://npm.pkg.github.com\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n[workflow file]: https://help.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions\n[npm authentication token]: https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-viewing-authentication-tokens\n[GitHub Package Registry]: https://docs.github.com/en/packages/working-with-a-github-packages-registry/working-with-the-npm-registry\n[good security practices]: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/secure-use#using-third-party-actions\n\n### Action usage\n\nYou can set any or all of the following input parameters using &#96;with&#96;:\n\n| Name | Type | Default | Description |\n| ---------------- | ---------------------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| &#96;token&#96; | string | None | Registry authentication token, not required if using [trusted publishing][]³ |\n| &#96;registry&#96;¹ | string | &#96;https://registry.npmjs.org/&#96; | Registry URL to use. |\n| &#96;package&#96; | string | Current working directory | Path to a package directory, a &#96;package.json&#96;, or a packed &#96;.tgz&#96; to publish. |\n| &#96;tag&#96;¹ | string | &#96;latest&#96; | [Distribution tag][npm-tag] to publish to. |\n| &#96;access&#96;¹ | &#96;public&#96;, &#96;restricted&#96; | [npm defaults][npm-access] | Whether the package should be publicly visible or restricted. |\n| &#96;provenance&#96;¹ ² | boolean | &#96;false&#96; | Run &#96;npm publish&#96; with the &#96;--provenance&#96; flag to add [provenance][] statements. |\n| &#96;strategy&#96; | &#96;all&#96;, &#96;upgrade&#96; | &#96;all&#96; | Use &#96;all&#96; to publish all unique versions, &#96;upgrade&#96; for only semver upgrades. |\n| &#96;ignore-scripts&#96; | boolean | &#96;true&#96; | Run &#96;npm publish&#96; with the &#96;--ignore-scripts&#96; flag as a security precaution. |\n| &#96;dry-run&#96; | boolean | &#96;false&#96; | Run &#96;npm publish&#96; with the &#96;--dry-run&#96; flag to prevent publication. |\n\n1. May be specified using &#96;publishConfig&#96; in &#96;package.json&#96;.\n2. Provenance requires npm &#96;\u003e=9.5.0&#96;.\n3. Trusted publishing npm &#96;\u003e=11.5.1&#96; and must be run from a supported cloud provider.\n\n[npm-tag]: https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v9/commands/npm-publish#tag\n[npm-access]: https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v9/commands/npm-publish#access\n[provenance]: https://docs.npmjs.com/generating-provenance-statements\n\n### Action output\n\nnpm-publish exposes several output variables, which you can use in later steps of your workflow if you provide an &#96;id&#96; for the npm-publish step.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n  steps:\n    - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n+     id: publish\n\n+   - if: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.type }}\n+     run: echo \"Version changed!\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Name | Type | Description |\n| ------------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| &#96;id&#96; | string | Package identifier of the release: &#96;${name}@${version}&#96; or empty if no release. |\n| &#96;type&#96; | string | [Semver release type][], &#96;initial&#96; if first release, &#96;different&#96; if other change, or empty if no release. |\n| &#96;name&#96; | string | Name of the package. |\n| &#96;version&#96; | string | Version of the package. |\n| &#96;old-version&#96; | string | Previously published version on &#96;tag&#96; or empty if no previous version on tag. |\n| &#96;tag&#96; | string | [Distribution tag][npm-tag] the package was published to. |\n| &#96;access&#96; | string | [Access level][npm-access] the package was published with, or &#96;default&#96; if scoped-package defaults were used. |\n| &#96;registry&#96; | string | Registry the package was published to. |\n| &#96;dry-run&#96; | boolean | Whether &#96;npm publish&#96; was run in \"dry run\" mode. |\n\n[semver release type]: https://github.com/npm/node-semver#release_types\n\n## JavaScript API\n\nTo use npm-package in your JavaScript code, you'll need to install it using [npm][] or other package manager of choice:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpm install --save-dev @jsdevtools/npm-publish\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can then import it and use it in your code like this:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\nimport { npmPublish } from \"@jsdevtools/npm-publish\";\n\n// Run npm-publish with all defaults\nawait npmPublish({ token: \"YOUR_NPM_AUTH_TOKEN_HERE\" });\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n[npm]: https://docs.npmjs.com/about-npm/\n\n### API usage\n\nAs shown in the example above, you should pass an options object to the &#96;npmPublish&#96; function. In TypeScript, the &#96;Options&#96; interface is available as an import.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ts\nimport type { Options } from \"@jsdevtools/npm-publish\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Name | Type | Default | Description |\n| -------------------- | ---------------------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| &#96;token&#96; | string | None | Registry authentication token, not required if using [trusted publishing][]³ |\n| &#96;registry&#96;¹ | string, &#96;URL&#96; | &#96;https://registry.npmjs.org/&#96; | Registry URL to use. |\n| &#96;package&#96; | string | Current working directory | Path to a package directory, a &#96;package.json&#96;, or a packed &#96;.tgz&#96; to publish. |\n| &#96;tag&#96;¹ | string | &#96;latest&#96; | [Distribution tag][npm-tag] to publish to. |\n| &#96;access&#96;¹ | &#96;public&#96;, &#96;restricted&#96; | [npm defaults][npm-access] | Whether the package should be publicly visible or restricted. |\n| &#96;provenance&#96;¹ ² | boolean | &#96;false&#96; | Run &#96;npm publish&#96; with the &#96;--provenance&#96; flag to add [provenance][] statements. |\n| &#96;strategy&#96; | &#96;all&#96;, &#96;upgrade&#96; | &#96;all&#96; | Use &#96;all&#96; to publish all unique versions, &#96;upgrade&#96; for only semver upgrades. |\n| &#96;ignoreScripts&#96; | boolean | &#96;true&#96; | Run &#96;npm publish&#96; with the &#96;--ignore-scripts&#96; flag as a security precaution. |\n| &#96;dryRun&#96; | boolean | &#96;false&#96; | Run &#96;npm publish&#96; with the &#96;--dry-run&#96; flag to prevent publication. |\n| &#96;logger&#96; | object | None | Logging interface with &#96;debug&#96;, &#96;info&#96;, and &#96;error&#96; log methods. |\n| &#96;temporaryDirectory&#96; | string | &#96;os.tmpdir()&#96; | Temporary directory to hold a generated &#96;.npmrc&#96; file |\n\n1. May be specified using &#96;publishConfig&#96; in &#96;package.json&#96;.\n2. Provenance requires npm &#96;\u003e=9.5.0&#96;.\n3. Trusted publishing npm &#96;\u003e=11.5.1&#96; and must be run from a supported cloud provider.\n\n### API output\n\nThe &#96;npmPublish()&#96; function returns a promise of a &#96;Results&#96; object. In TypeScript, the &#96;Results&#96; interface is available as an import.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ts\nimport type { Results } from \"@jsdevtools/npm-publish\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Name | Type | Description |\n| ------------ | --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| &#96;id&#96; | Optional string | Package identifier of the release: &#96;${name}@${version}&#96; or &#96;undefined&#96; if no release. |\n| &#96;type&#96; | Optional string | [Semver release type][], &#96;initial&#96; if first release, &#96;different&#96; if other change, or &#96;undefined&#96; if no release. |\n| &#96;name&#96; | string | Name of the package. |\n| &#96;version&#96; | string | Version of the package. |\n| &#96;oldVersion&#96; | Optional string | Previously published version on &#96;tag&#96; or &#96;undefined&#96; if no previous version. |\n| &#96;tag&#96; | string | [Distribution tag][npm-tag] that the package was published to. |\n| &#96;access&#96; | Optional string | [Access level][npm-access] the package was published with, or &#96;undefined&#96; if scoped-package defaults were used. |\n| &#96;registry&#96; | &#96;URL&#96; | Registry the package was published to. |\n| &#96;dryRun&#96; | boolean | Whether &#96;npm publish&#96; was run in \"dry run\" mode. |\n\n## Command Line Interface\n\nYou can also use &#96;npm-publish&#96; as a command-line tool in your terminal.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpm install --save-dev @jsdevtools/npm-publish\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can then use it in your terminal or in &#96;npm run&#96; scripts.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpx npm-publish --token YOUR_NPM_AUTH_TOKEN_HERE\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can customize your call with options to change the registry, package, etc.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpx npm-publish --token YOUR_NPM_AUTH_TOKEN_HERE --registry http://example.com ./path/to/package\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Options\n\nRun &#96;npm-publish --help&#96; to see the full list of options available.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nUsage:\n\n  npm-publish \u003coptions\u003e [package]\n\nArguments:\n\n  package                 The path to the package to publish.\n                          May be a directory, package.json, or .tgz file.\n                          Defaults to the package in the current directory.\n\nOptions:\n\n  --token \u003ctoken\u003e         npm authentication token.\n                          Not required if using trusted publishing.\n                          See npm documentation for details.\n\n  --registry \u003curl\u003e        Registry to read from and write to.\n                          Defaults to \"https://registry.npmjs.org/\".\n\n  --tag \u003ctag\u003e             The distribution tag to check against and publish to.\n                          Defaults to \"latest\".\n\n  --access \u003caccess\u003e       Package access, may be \"public\" or \"restricted\".\n                          See npm documentation for details.\n\n  --provenance            Publish with provenance statements.\n                          See npm documentation for details.\n\n  --strategy \u003cstrategy\u003e   Publish strategy, may be \"all\" or \"upgrade\".\n                          Defaults to \"all\", see documentation for details.\n\n  --no-ignore-scripts     Allow lifecycle scripts, which are disabled by default\n                          as a security precaution. Defaults to false.\n\n  --dry-run               Do not actually publish anything.\n  --quiet                 Only print errors.\n  --debug                 Print debug logs.\n\n  -v, --version           Print the version number.\n  -h, --help              Show usage text.\n\nExamples:\n\n  $ npm-publish --token abc123 ./my-package\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Migration guides\n\nMajor releases of the action and libraries may contain breaking changes, documented here.\nFor more detailed change logs, see [releases][].\n\n### v3 to v4\n\nThe &#96;v4&#96; release does not require any changes to how you use the &#96;npm-publish&#96; action from &#96;v3&#96;. The action was updated to Node 24 / npm 11.\n\nIn the library and CLI, support for Node 16 and Node 18 was dropped in &#96;v4&#96;, and the library API was switched to ESM-only. Library users should switch to ESM or update Node to a version with support for [loading ES modules using &#96;require&#96;][esm-require].\n\n[esm-require]: https://nodejs.org/api/modules.html#loading-ecmascript-modules-using-require\n\n### v2 to v4\n\nThe &#96;v4&#96; release does not require any changes to how you use the &#96;npm-publish&#96; action from &#96;v2&#96;. The action was updated to Node 20 in &#96;v3&#96; due to GitHub Action's [deprecation of Node 16][node16-deprecation], and then updated to Node 24 in &#96;v4&#96;.\n\nIn the library and CLI, support for Node 16 and Node 18 was dropped in &#96;v4&#96;, and the library API was switched to ESM-only. Library users should switch to ESM or update Node to a version with support for [loading ES modules using &#96;require&#96;][esm-require].\n\n[node16-deprecation]: https://github.blog/changelog/2023-09-22-github-actions-transitioning-from-node-16-to-node-20/\n\n### v1 to v4\n\nThe v2 release made several breaking changes to inputs, outputs, and behaviors that were present in &#96;v1&#96;. The examples below focus on the action, but the same changes are applicable to the library and CLI, too.\n\nIn the library and CLI, support for Node 16 and Node 18 was dropped in &#96;v4&#96;, and the library API was switched to ESM-only. Library users should switch to ESM or update Node to a version with support for [loading ES modules using &#96;require&#96;][esm-require].\n\n#### option changes\n\nThe &#96;check-version&#96; and &#96;greater-version-only&#96; boolean options were replaced with the &#96;strategy&#96; option:\n\n- &#96;strategy: all&#96; (default) will publish any version that does not yet exist in the registry\n- &#96;strategy: upgrade&#96; will publish only if the version is a semver upgrade of the requested &#96;dist-tag&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n  with:\n    token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n-   check-version: true\n-   greater-version-only: false\n+   strategy: all\n\n  with:\n    token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n-   check-version: true\n-   greater-version-only: true\n+   strategy: upgrade\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;check-version: false&#96; has been removed. If you only need to publish, without first checking whether the version exists in the registry, you can [use &#96;npm&#96; directly][publishing-nodejs-packages] instead:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n  - uses: actions/setup-node@v6\n    with:\n      node-version: '24'\n+     registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org/\n\n- - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v1\n-   with:\n-     token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n-     check-version: false\n+ - run: npm publish\n+   env:\n+     NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n[publishing-nodejs-packages]: https://docs.github.com/actions/publishing-packages/publishing-nodejs-packages\n\n#### output changes\n\nThe &#96;type&#96; output is now an empty string instead of &#96;'none'&#96; when no release occurs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n  - run: echo \"Version changed!\"\n-   if: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.type != 'none' }}\n+   if: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.type }}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### behavior changes\n\nThe &#96;--ignore-scripts&#96; option is now passed to &#96;npm publish&#96; as a security precaution. If you define any publish lifecycle scripts - &#96;prepublishOnly&#96;, &#96;prepack&#96;, &#96;prepare&#96;, &#96;postpack&#96;, &#96;publish&#96;, &#96;postpublish&#96; - we recommend you run that logic as a separate explicit build step.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n+ - run: npm run build\n\n- - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v1\n+ - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n    with:\n      token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf you can't change your build, you can set the &#96;ignore-scripts&#96; input to &#96;false&#96; as a workaround. Be aware that failures during a lifecycle script can be difficult to debug, and any &#96;stdout&#96;/&#96;stderr&#96; output from your build script could interfere with how &#96;npm-publish&#96; interprets results from the &#96;npm&#96; CLI.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n- - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v1\n+ - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n    with:\n      token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n+     ignore-scripts: false\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe global &#96;.npmrc&#96; file is no longer read nor modified. This means the &#96;token&#96; option is now required for the library and CLI. (It was already required for the action.) You may have workarounds in place referencing &#96;INPUT_TOKEN&#96;, which v1 [erroneously wrote][#15] to &#96;.npmrc&#96;. These workarounds should be removed.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;diff\n  - uses: actions/setup-node@v6\n    with:\n      node-version: '24'\n      registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org/\n\n- - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v1\n+ - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4\n    with:\n      token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n\n  - name: Do some more stuff with npm\n    run: npm whoami\n    env:\n-     INPUT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n+     NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n[#15]: https://github.com/JS-DevTools/npm-publish/issues/15\n\n## License\n\nnpm-publish is 100% free and open-source, under the [MIT license](LICENSE). Use it however you want.\n\nThis package is [Treeware](http://treeware.earth). If you use it in production, then we ask that you [**buy the world a tree**](https://plant.treeware.earth/JS-DevTools/npm-publish) to thank us for our work. By contributing to the Treeware forest you’ll be creating employment for local families and restoring wildlife habitats.\n\n## Big Thanks To\n\nThanks to these awesome companies for their support of Open Source developers ❤\n\n[![GitHub](https://jstools.dev/img/badges/github.svg)](https://github.com/open-source)\n[![NPM](https://jstools.dev/img/badges/npm.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/)\n[![Coveralls](https://jstools.dev/img/badges/coveralls.svg)](https://coveralls.io)\n",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JS-DevTools/npm-publish/main/README.md"
}
```
User decision: **accepted**

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
已理清问题全貌。

正在分析 `deploy.yml` 的根因。工作流未向 `JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4` 传入 `token`，仅依赖 OIDC 信任发布。这导致 npm 因缺少有效认证而返回 `ENEEDAUTH` 错误。

最可能的原因是 npm 包未配置 Trusted Publisher。虽然工作流开启了 OIDC，但 npmjs.com 上的 `rd-component` 必须将该 GitHub 仓库关联为可信发布者，否则 OIDC 认证会失败。

另外，工作流中没有配置 `NPM_TOKEN` 秘密作为回退方案。同时，`setup-node` 写入了 registry-url，却未提供 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`，这会让 npm 配置产生误导。</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

## 错误含义

`ENEEDAUTH` 表示执行 `npm publish` 时，**npm 没有拿到有效的 registry 认证**。  
Workflow 已跑到发布步骤，但 npm 认为当前机器未登录 `https://registry.npmjs.org/`。

---

## 当前 Workflow 配置

```1:42:e:\dolphin\rd-component\.github\workflows\deploy.yml
name: rd-component-publish

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write   
    steps:
      ...
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'  
      ...
      - uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4
```

要点：

1. 已设置 `id-token: write`（为 OIDC Trusted Publishing 准备）
2. **没有** 给 `npm-publish` 传 `token`
3. **没有** 在仓库 Secrets 里配置 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` / `NPM_TOKEN`
4. `package.json` 里也没有 `publishConfig`

因此当前走的是 **OIDC Trusted Publishing**，而不是传统 npm Token。

---

## 最可能的原因（按概率排序）

### 1. npm 上未配置 Trusted Publisher（最常见）

Workflow 只写了 `id-token: write`，但 **npm 包页面还必须手动绑定 GitHub 仓库**，OIDC 才会生效。

需要在 [npmjs.com](https://www.npmjs.com/) 上：

1. 打开包 `rd-component` → **Settings** → **Trusted Publishers**
2. 添加 **GitHub Actions** 类型的 Trusted Publisher
3. 填写：
   - **Organization / User**：GitHub 仓库所属 org 或用户
   - **Repository**：`rd-component`（或实际仓库名）
   - **Workflow filename**：`deploy.yml`（必须与 `.github/workflows/` 下文件名完全一致）
   - **Environment**（可选）：若 workflow 未指定 environment，通常留空

若未配置、或 repository / workflow 文件名填错，GitHub 即使能签发 OIDC token，npm 也不会接受 → 出现 `ENEEDAUTH`。

---

### 2. 未配置 NPM Token，且 Trusted Publishing 也未生效

`JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4` 的认证方式只有两种：

| 方式 | 当前状态 |
|------|----------|
| OIDC Trusted Publishing | 有 `id-token: write`，但 npm 端可能未配置 |
| `token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}` | **未配置** |

若团队之前用 Classic Token 发布，而 npm 已停用/撤销 Classic Token，又未迁移到 Trusted Publishing，就会直接认证失败。

**传统 Token 方式**需要在 GitHub 仓库 Settings → Secrets 添加 `NPM_TOKEN`（Automation 或 Granular Token，具备 publish 权限），并在 workflow 中：

```yaml
- uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
```

---

### 3. npm 版本不满足 Trusted Publishing 要求

Trusted Publishing 需要 **npm >= 11.5.1**。  
当前 workflow 使用 `node-version: 22`，默认自带的 npm 往往是 **10.x**，可能不支持 OIDC。

`npm-publish@v4` 内部会调用 runner 上的 `npm publish`；若 npm 过旧，OIDC 无法工作，也会表现为 `ENEEDAUTH`。

可在 publish 前增加一步验证（排查用）：

```yaml
- run: npm --version
```

若低于 11.5.1，需要升级 npm，例如：

```yaml
- run: npm install -g npm@latest
```

---

### 4. 存在空的或无效的 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` Secret

若仓库或 Organization 里配置了 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` 但值为空、过期或无效：

- `setup-node` 的 `registry-url` 会写入 `~/.npmrc`
- npm 可能优先尝试该 token，而不是 OIDC
- 空 token 会 **阻断** OIDC 回退

排查：GitHub → Settings → Secrets → 检查是否存在空的 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` / `NPM_TOKEN`。

---

### 5. 私有包缺少只读 Token（若包为 private）

若 `rd-component` 是 **private** 包，即使用 Trusted Publishing，`npm-publish` 在发布前查询 registry 版本时仍可能需要只读 token：

```yaml
- uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}  # 只读即可
```

公开包一般不需要；当前版本 `0.1.191` 说明包很可能已在 npm 上存在，此项概率较低。

---

### 6. `setup-node` 的 `registry-url` 容易造成误解

你配置了：

```yaml
registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
```

这通常配合 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` 使用。但 `npm-publish@v4` **不会读取** `~/.npmrc`，而是自行处理认证（token 或 OIDC）。

因此：**仅有 `registry-url` 不会自动完成认证**；它不能替代 Trusted Publisher 或 `NPM_TOKEN`。

---

## 排查建议

1. **在 npm 包设置中确认 Trusted Publisher**  
   核对 repository、workflow 文件名 `deploy.yml` 是否与 GitHub 一致。

2. **查看 GitHub Actions 完整日志**  
   - 若 `npm-publish` 在版本检查阶段就失败 → 可能是私有包缺 token  
   - 若在 `npm publish` 阶段失败 → 基本是发布认证问题（本次即此类）

3. **检查 Secrets**  
   - 是否有 `NPM_TOKEN` / `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`  
   - 是否为空或已过期

4. **确认 npm 版本**  
   在 publish 步骤前打印 `npm --version`，需 >= 11.5.1（若走 OIDC）。

5. **确认发布账号权限**  
   执行 `pnpm publish` 的 npm 账号（或 Token 对应账号）必须是 `rd-component` 的 maintainer。

---

## 两种修复路径（二选一）

### 方案 A：OIDC Trusted Publishing（推荐，与当前 workflow 一致）

1. 在 npm 包页面配置 Trusted Publisher（见上文）
2. 确保 npm >= 11.5.1
3. **不要**设置空的 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`
4. 保持 workflow 中 `id-token: write`，不传 `token`

### 方案 B：传统 NPM Token（更简单、兼容性更好）

1. 在 npm 创建 Granular Access Token（Publish 权限，限定包 `rd-component`）
2. 写入 GitHub Secret：`NPM_TOKEN`
3. 修改 workflow：

```yaml
- uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
```

若同时使用 OIDC，**不要**再设置 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`，避免与 OIDC 冲突。

---

## 总结

| 现象 | 原因 |
|------|------|
| `ENEEDAUTH` | CI 执行 `npm publish` 时没有有效认证 |
| Workflow 有 `id-token: write` 但无 token | 依赖 OIDC，需在 npm 端配置 Trusted Publisher |
| 有 `registry-url` 但无 Secret | `setup-node` 不会自动提供 token |
| Node 22 默认 npm 可能偏旧 | OIDC 需要 npm >= 11.5.1 |

**最可能的原因**：npm 包 `rd-component` 尚未配置（或配置错误）GitHub Actions Trusted Publisher，同时仓库里也没有有效的 `NPM_TOKEN`。

若你方便提供：该包在 npm 上是 public 还是 private、以及 GitHub 仓库完整路径（`owner/repo`），我可以帮你对照 Trusted Publisher 应填的具体字段。

## 为什么之前还会 `ENEEDAUTH`？

最可能的原因：**Trusted Publisher 当时还没保存/生效**。

OIDC 认证只有在 npm 端完成绑定后才会工作。若 CI 跑 publish 时 npm 上还没有这条 Trusted Publisher 记录，GitHub 即使签发了 OIDC token，npm 也会拒绝 → `ENEEDAUTH`。

---

## 接下来请按顺序做

### 1. 保存 Trusted Publisher
点击表单底部的 **Add** / **Save**（截图里还没看到是否已提交）。保存后 npm 会显示已绑定的 publisher 列表。

### 2. 确认 GitHub 仓库路径
仓库必须是 **`RedDwarfTech/rd-component`**。若实际在别的 org 或用户名下，表单里的 Organization 要改成真实 owner。

### 3. 检查 Secrets 是否干扰 OIDC
在 GitHub 仓库 **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions** 中确认：
- **没有**空的 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` 或 `NPM_TOKEN`
- 若存在但已过期/无效，可能干扰 OIDC，建议删除或更新

### 4. 确保 npm 版本支持 Trusted Publishing
Trusted Publishing 需要 **npm >= 11.5.1**。Node 22 默认 npm 可能是 10.x，建议在 publish 前升级：

```yaml
- run: npm install -g npm@latest
```

放在 `JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4` 之前即可。

### 5. 重新触发 workflow
保存 Trusted Publisher 后，**重新 push 到 `main`**，或在 Actions 里 **Re-run workflow**。  
当前 `package.json` 版本是 `0.1.191`，npm 上最新是 `0.1.190`，版本有变化，满足发布条件。

---

## 若保存后仍失败

按优先级排查：

1. **npm 版本** — 日志里看 `npm --version` 是否 >= 11.5.1  
2. **Secrets 冲突** — 是否存在无效 `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`  
3. **备用方案** — 在 GitHub 添加 `NPM_TOKEN`，并在 workflow 中显式传入：

```yaml
- uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v4
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
```

---

**总结**：截图配置本身没问题，关键是 **先保存 Trusted Publisher，再重新跑 CI**。若保存后仍报错，优先检查 npm 版本和 Secrets 冲突。需要我帮你改 workflow（例如加 npm 升级步骤）的话，请切换到 Agent 模式。

## 进展：认证问题已解决

从日志可以确认 **Trusted Publishing / OIDC 已经生效**：

- `Signed provenance statement with source and build information from GitHub Actions`
- Provenance 已写入 transparency log

之前的 `ENEEDAUTH` 已解决，当前是 **新的校验错误**。

---

## 当前错误原因

```
E422 Unprocessable Entity
Error verifying sigstore provenance bundle: Failed to validate repository information:
package.json: "repository.url" is "",
expected to match "https://github.com/RedDwarfTech/rd-component" from provenance
```

含义：

1. GitHub Actions 发布时自动生成了 **provenance**（来源证明）
2. Provenance 里记录的仓库是 `https://github.com/RedDwarfTech/rd-component`
3. 但 `package.json` 里 **没有** `repository` 字段（`repository.url` 为空）
4. npm 校验两者必须一致 → 拒绝发布

当前 `package.json` 只有 `name`、`version` 等，缺少 `repository`：

```1:5:e:\dolphin\rd-component\package.json
{
    "name": "rd-component",
    "version": "0.1.191",
    "type": "module",
    "description": "Reddwarf public component lib",
```

---

## 修复方法

在 `package.json` 顶层添加 `repository` 字段，例如：

```json
"repository": {
  "type": "git",
  "url": "https://github.com/RedDwarfTech/rd-component.git"
}
```

或使用 npm 简写：

```json
"repository": "github:RedDwarfTech/rd-component"
```

两种写法 npm 都会解析为同一仓库，需与 Trusted Publisher 配置的 `RedDwarfTech/rd-component` 一致。

建议同时加上（可选但推荐）：

```json
"bugs": {
  "url": "https://github.com/RedDwarfTech/rd-component/issues"
},
"homepage": "https://github.com/RedDwarfTech/rd-component#readme"
```

修改后 commit 并 push 到 `main`，重新触发 workflow 即可。

---

## 关于那些 `npm warn`（不是失败原因）

```
Unknown project config "auto-install-peers"
Unknown project config "resolve-peers-from-workspace-root"
...
```

这些来自项目根目录的 `.npmrc`，是 **pnpm 专用配置**。`npm publish` 会读取 `.npmrc` 但不认识这些项，所以告警。  
**不会导致 E422**，以后 npm 大版本可能移除对未知配置的支持，可暂时忽略，或把 pnpm 配置移到 `pnpm-workspace.yaml` / `.pnpmfile.cjs` 等 pnpm 专用位置。

---

## 总结

| 阶段 | 状态 |
|------|------|
| OIDC / Trusted Publishing | ✅ 已成功 |
| Provenance 签名 | ✅ 已成功 |
| `package.json` 仓库信息 | ❌ 缺失，导致 E422 |

**只需在 `package.json` 添加正确的 `repository` 字段**，与 `RedDwarfTech/rd-component` 匹配，再重新发布。
